trajectory_summary: The call books cleanly and Terry ends warm, but the warmth is largely Terry's own disposition — Bucky's verify section is a rapid checklist, the bill anchor is never flipped emotionally, and the wife's consistent role as the financial voice in the household is never converted into a confirmed appointment inclusion. The rapport exchange in the middle of the call (ministry, cremation, scripture) is genuine and Bucky participates authentically, which earns real credit — but it also consumes time that could have been used to deepen the bill anchor or confirm the wife's presence on Monday.
outcome_dispute: The appointment is booked and Terry is enthusiastic. However, the wife is on the title, answered every financial question on the call, and was never confirmed as a participant in the Monday closer appointment. Per protocols.spouse_decision, this is a structurally vulnerable booking — not a clean win.

primary_coaching_focus.why: The wife answered every financial question on this call — bill amount, credit score, bill delivery method — and was never confirmed as a participant in Monday's closer appointment. Per protocols.spouse_decision, this is a hard protocol requirement, not a soft coaching note. The closer walks into Monday blind to a co-decision-maker who was more financially engaged than the person Bucky was talking to. This is the single most actionable fix from this call: Bucky needs to add one line at button-up — 'And we'll make sure your wife is on the call with us Monday, right?' — and practice it until it's automatic.

key_moments.stewart_read: Terry confirms his wife is on the title at 01:06. Bucky acknowledges with 'Okay' and moves immediately to the next verify question. The wife then answers every financial question on the call — bill amount, credit score, bill delivery method — yet Bucky never confirms she will be on the Monday closer appointment. The schema is explicit: spouse on title must be included in the appointment, not overcome as an objection. Bucky never even raises the question.

key_moments.quote: What's your interest in solar? Saving some money?
key_moments.stewart_read: This is the verify section's highest-leverage question and Bucky supplies the answer before Terry can. Terry does surface his own language — 'with the utility bills as they are' at 01:29 — but Bucky never develops it. The bill is captured as a number ($165/month) and dropped. The schema's capture-acknowledge-flip pattern is not executed; Bucky moves straight to utility company. The emotional alignment-against-utility that should anchor the entire pitch never happens.

key_moments.quote: I am talking to another guy that, somebody in my church had already used their company.
key_moments.stewart_read: Terry discloses an active competitor — a church referral, which carries social trust weight that a cold quote does not. Bucky's response at 02:43 ('Just see what you can get, what options you have?') is a paraphrase of Terry's own words, not a reframe. The schema's already_saw_design strategy calls for comparison framing: 'We offer different equipment than most companies and our price points can be different.' Bucky doesn't deploy it. The church-referral context makes this more significant than a generic competitor mention — the social proof is working against Ion, and Bucky doesn't acknowledge it.

key_moments.quote: One of the reasons I called was, you know, we're probably looking at having a a tree trimmer come out after this Okay. Windstorm we had and take down at least a dead tree. And if if if you all felt like there was another tree or a tree that needed to be seriously trimmed or something, it would be helpful for me to know that upfront.
key_moments.stewart_read: Terry is volunteering a shading concern after the appointment is already booked — this is proactive investment in the design's accuracy. More importantly, tree removal improves sun exposure, which is a direct qualifier upgrade. Bucky's response ('They might. They'll talk to you about it. I'll put it in a note') is procedurally adequate but misses the enthusiasm signal entirely. Terry is telling Bucky he is actively managing the conditions for solar to work. That deserves acknowledgment and amplification, not a note.
